concept_name: A Bar Magnet in a Uniform Field (τ = m × B, U = −m·B, T = 2π√(I/mB))
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics Part 1, Chapter 5 (Magnetism and Matter) §5.2.3 (The dipole in a uniform magnetic field) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. NCERT consulted for the Indian real-world anchor (the compass needle and the freely-suspended bar magnet / vibration magnetometer). The atomic concept taught here is what a UNIFORM external field B does to a bar magnet of magnetic moment m: each pole feels a force (the N pole +mB along B, the S pole −mB opposite), and because these forces are equal, opposite, and act on different lines they form a COUPLE — the net force is ZERO (the magnet does not translate) but the couple produces a torque τ = m × B of magnitude mB·sinθ that rotates the magnet toward alignment with B. The torque is maximum at θ = 90° (a cross product is largest when the vectors are perpendicular) and zero at θ = 0° and θ = 180°. Released near alignment the magnet OSCILLATES about θ = 0° with period T = 2π√(I/mB), where I is its moment of inertia — the principle of the vibration magnetometer. The potential energy is U = −m·B = −mB·cosθ: a MINIMUM at θ = 0° (stable equilibrium — a nudge produces a restoring torque) and a MAXIMUM at θ = 180° (unstable — any nudge grows and the magnet flips to θ = 0°). The deepest student confusions targeted: (M1) a uniform field pushes the magnet bodily along B (net force is zero — only a torque); (M2) the magnet feels no force at all (each pole DOES feel mB; they form a couple); (M3) torque is maximum when m ∥ B (it is zero there; maximum at 90°); (M4) θ = 180° is as stable as θ = 0° (it is the energy maximum — unstable); (M5) the oscillation period is independent of the field (T = 2π√(I/mB), so a stronger m or B gives a faster swing). This is the magnetic twin of electric_dipole_in_field (τ = p × E, U = −p·E), reusing its rotational-dynamics renderer. CUT-LINE: the bar magnet's own field / closed-loop lines / no-monopole / axial-equatorial 1/r³ is the prerequisite bar_magnet_as_dipole; Gauss's law for magnetism (∮B·dA = 0) is the downstream gauss_law_magnetism. Conceptual-only ship (no mode_overrides). EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; the five misconceptions are confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + predict→reveal).

real_world_anchor.primary: Picture the magnetic compass needle from your geography or physics class. The needle is a tiny bar magnet sitting in the Earth's uniform field. Let it go and it swings until it points along the field, then holds there; push it aside and it springs straight back. That 'springs back to alignment' is exactly the torque τ = mB·sinθ doing its job — and a bar magnet in any uniform field behaves the very same way.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture the vibration magnetometer used in the school lab — a small bar magnet hung by a thread that you twist and release, then count how many swings it makes per minute. The time for one swing is the period T = 2π√(I/mB); a stronger magnet or a stronger field makes it swing faster. That timed swing is how the lab actually measures a magnetic field.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture a bar magnet floating on a cork in a bowl of water, free to turn. It rotates until its N pole points roughly north, lining up with the Earth's field, and settles there — the same τ = m × B settling the magnet into its lowest-energy pose.

m.name: Magnetic moment of the bar magnet

B.name: Magnitude of the uniform external field

I.name: Moment of inertia of the magnet

formulas.torque_vector: τ = m × B — the vector torque is the cross product of the magnetic moment with the field; it is perpendicular to both m and B.
formulas.torque_magnitude: τ = mB·sinθ — scalar magnitude; θ is the angle between m and B; maximum at θ = 90°, zero at θ = 0° or 180°.
formulas.net_force_zero: F_net = (+mB) + (−mB) = 0 in a UNIFORM field — the two pole-forces are equal and opposite, so the magnet rotates but does not translate.
formulas.potential_energy: U = −m·B = −mB·cosθ — minimum (−mB) at θ = 0°, zero at θ = 90°, maximum (+mB) at θ = 180°.
formulas.oscillation_period: T = 2π√(I/mB) — released near alignment the magnet oscillates about θ = 0° with this period (I = moment of inertia); a stronger m or B gives a faster swing.
formulas.equilibrium_stable: θ = 0° (m ∥ B) — stable equilibrium; U is a minimum, and a small nudge produces a restoring torque toward alignment.
formulas.equilibrium_unstable: θ = 180° (m anti-parallel to B) — unstable equilibrium; U is a maximum, and any tiny nudge grows until the magnet flips to θ = 0°.

constraints: B must be uniform across the magnet. A non-uniform field adds a net force (gradient term) so the magnet would also translate.
The magnet is rigid — its magnetic moment m and length are fixed during rotation.
τ = m × B is a vector perpendicular to BOTH m and B; its magnitude is mB·sinθ.
θ is measured from m to B; a small positive θ gives a restoring torque toward θ = 0°.
U = −m·B is defined up to a constant; the standard reference is U = 0 at θ = 90°.
The small-oscillation period T = 2π√(I/mB) holds for small amplitudes about the stable equilibrium θ = 0°.

aha_moment.statement: Net force is zero, yet the torque is not — the magnet turns without moving.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: Watch STATE_3 → STATE_4: the two equal-and-opposite pole forces resolve to a ΣF = 0 badge, yet the τ vector appears and the magnet begins to turn. Both facts are true at once.

STATE_1.title: A bar magnet and its moment m (S → N)

scene_composition.text: A bar magnet: N (red) and S (blue), with its moment m

The magnetic moment m
points from S to N.
Its size measures how
strong the magnet is.

No external field yet.
Next: drop this magnet
into a uniform field B.
A compass needle is exactly this — a tiny bar magnet.

tts_sentences.text_en: This is a bar magnet. North pole red, south pole blue, with its magnetic moment m drawn in yellow.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह एक bar magnet है। North pole red, south pole blue, इसके magnetic moment m के साथ जो yellow रंग में है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది ఒక bar magnet. North pole red, south pole blue, దాని magnetic moment m yellow రంగులో గీయబడింది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The yellow arrow is the magnetic moment m. It points from the south pole to the north pole.
tts_sentences.text_hi: yellow arrow magnetic moment m है। यह south pole से north pole की तरफ point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: yellow arrow magnetic moment m. ఇది south pole నుండి north pole వైపు point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Its size measures how strong the magnet is. There is no field yet.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसका size बताता है कि magnet कितना मज़बूत है। अभी कोई field नहीं है।
tts_sentences.text_te: దాని size magnet ఎంత బలంగా ఉందో చెబుతుంది. ఇంకా field లేదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Next, we drop this magnet into a uniform external magnetic field B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अगले step में, हम इस magnet को एक uniform external magnetic field B में डालेंगे।
tts_sentences.text_te: తర్వాత, మనం ఈ magnet ని ఒక uniform external magnetic field B లో వేస్తాం.

STATE_2.title: Place it in a uniform field B

scene_composition.text: A uniform field B — the same everywhere

Every blue arrow is identical:
same length, same direction.
That is what 'uniform' means.

The magnet sits at an angle θ
to the field. Watch the yellow
arc between m and B.
What will the field do to this tilted magnet?

tts_sentences.text_en: Now switch on a uniform magnetic field. Every blue arrow is identical in length and direction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब एक uniform magnetic field switch on करते हैं। हर blue arrow length और direction में identical है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు ఒక uniform magnetic field switch on చేద్దాం. ప్రతి blue arrow length మరియు direction లో identical.

tts_sentences.text_en: Uniform means the field is the same at every point. It does not get stronger or weaker anywhere.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Uniform का मतलब field हर जगह एक समान है। यह कहीं stronger या weaker नहीं होती।
tts_sentences.text_te: Uniform అంటే field అన్ని చోట్లా ఒకేలా ఉంటుంది. ఇది ఎక్కడా stronger లేదా weaker కాదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: The magnet sits tilted at an angle theta to the field. The yellow arc marks that angle between the magnetic moment m and the field B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Magnet field के साथ theta angle पर tilted है। yellow arc उस angle को mark करता है जो magnetic moment m और field B के बीच है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Magnet field తో theta angle లో tilted గా ఉంది. yellow arc magnetic moment m మరియు field B మధ్య ఆ angle ని చూపిస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Question. What does the field do to this tilted magnet? Keep watching.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Question। यह field इस tilted magnet के साथ क्या करती है? देखते रहो।
tts_sentences.text_te: Question. ఈ field ఈ tilted magnet కి ఏమి చేస్తుందో? చూస్తూ ఉండండి.

STATE_3.title: Two pole forces +mB and −mB — a couple

scene_composition.text: Each pole feels a force in the field

On N: force +mB,
along the field.
On S: force −mB,
opposite the field.

Equal. Opposite. But on
different lines of action.
That is a COUPLE.
Each force has magnitude mB. They do not cancel as a pair — they twist.

tts_sentences.text_en: Look at each pole separately. The north pole feels a force m B, pointing along the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर pole को अलग-अलग देखो। North pole को एक force m B महसूस होता है, जो field की direction में point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి pole ని విడిగా చూడండి. North pole కి ఒక force m B అనిపిస్తుంది, అది field వైపు point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The south pole feels a force m B pointing the opposite way, against the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: South pole को भी force m B महसूस होता है, लेकिन opposite direction में, field के against।
tts_sentences.text_te: South pole కి కూడా force m B అనిపిస్తుంది, కానీ opposite వైపు, field కి వ్యతిరేకంగా.

tts_sentences.text_en: Two forces. Equal in size. Opposite in direction. But acting on different lines. That combination is called a couple.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दो forces। size में बराबर। direction में opposite। लेकिन अलग-अलग lines पर act करती हैं। इस combination को couple कहते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండు forces. size లో సమానం. direction లో opposite. కానీ వేర్వేరు lines పై act చేస్తాయి. ఈ combination ని couple అంటారు.

tts_sentences.text_en: So the magnet does feel forces. It is just that the two forces are set up to twist it, not to push it.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो magnet को forces ज़रूर महसूस होती हैं। बस दोनों forces उसे push नहीं, बल्कि twist करने के लिए set up हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి magnet కి forces అనిపిస్తాయే. కేవలం ఆ రెండు forces దాన్ని push చేయవు, twist చేయడానికి set up అయ్యాయి.

misconception_watch.belief: A magnet placed in a field feels no force at all, because the poles are equal and opposite.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Each pole DOES feel a force mB. The two are equal and opposite, so they form a couple — zero net force, but a real twisting torque.

STATE_4.title: Net force zero, but torque is not

scene_composition.text: ΣF = 0, yet the magnet turns

Add the two forces:
+mB and −mB cancel.
ΣF = 0 → no sliding.

But the couple makes a torque.
The purple arrow is τ.
The magnet rotates toward B.
Zero net force and non-zero torque are both true at once. The magnet turns without moving off the spot.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now add the two forces together. Plus m B and minus m B cancel exactly. The net force is zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब दोनों forces को जोड़ो। plus m B और minus m B बिल्कुल cancel हो जाती हैं। net force zero हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు రెండు forces ని కలపండి. plus m B మరియు minus m B పూర్తిగా cancel అవుతాయి. net force zero అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Zero net force means the magnet will not slide anywhere. Take three seconds — does that mean nothing happens?
tts_sentences.text_hi: Zero net force का मतलब magnet कहीं slide नहीं करेगा। तीन seconds लो — क्या इसका मतलब है कुछ नहीं होता?
tts_sentences.text_te: Zero net force అంటే magnet ఎక్కడికీ slide అవ్వదు. మూడు seconds తీసుకోండి — దీని అర్థం ఏమీ జరగదా?

tts_sentences.text_en: No. The couple produces a torque. The purple arrow is the torque tau, and the magnet begins to rotate toward the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: नहीं। couple एक torque produce करता है। purple arrow torque tau है, और magnet field की तरफ rotate होना शुरू हो जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: లేదు. couple ఒక torque produce చేస్తుంది. purple arrow torque tau, మరియు magnet field వైపు rotate అవ్వడం మొదలవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Net force zero, but torque not zero. Both statements are true at the same moment.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Net force zero, लेकिन torque zero नहीं। दोनों बातें एक ही moment पर true हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: Net force zero, కానీ torque zero కాదు. రెండు statements ఒకే moment లో true గా ఉంటాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Forces decide sliding. Torques decide turning. A uniform field gives the magnet only a turn.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Forces sliding decide करती हैं। Torques turning decide करते हैं। एक uniform field magnet को सिर्फ़ एक turn देती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Forces sliding decide చేస్తాయి. Torques turning decide చేస్తాయి. ఒక uniform field magnet కి కేవలం ఒక turn ఇస్తుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: A uniform field pushes the magnet bodily along the direction of the field.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: In a UNIFORM field the net force is exactly zero — the magnet only rotates to align with B, it does not translate.

STATE_5.title: τ = mB·sinθ — sweep θ and watch τ trace a sine

scene_composition.text: Watch θ sweep — τ rises and falls

τ is zero when m lines up with B,
grows as the magnet tilts,
and falls back to zero again.

Functional form:
τ = mB·sinθ.
This is a sine curve.
Watch only. The purple τ arrow grows and shrinks with the angle.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the angle sweep from zero through ninety to one eighty and back. Keep your eye on the purple torque arrow.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Angle को zero से ninety होते हुए one eighty तक और वापस sweep होते देखो। purple torque arrow पर नज़र रखो।
tts_sentences.text_te: Angle zero నుండి ninety గుండా one eighty వరకు మరియు తిరిగి sweep అవ్వడం చూడండి. purple torque arrow పై దృష్టి పెట్టండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The torque starts at zero, grows as the magnet tilts away from the field, peaks, then falls back to zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Torque zero से शुरू होता है, जैसे-जैसे magnet field से tilt होता है बढ़ता है, peak करता है, फिर zero पर वापस आ जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Torque zero నుండి మొదలవుతుంది, magnet field నుండి tilt అవుతూ పెరుగుతుంది, peak చేరుతుంది, తర్వాత zero కి తిరిగి వస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That shape is a sine. Tau equals m B sine theta. Where does it reach its biggest value? You will guess next.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह shape एक sine है। tau equals m B sine theta। यह अपनी सबसे बड़ी value कहाँ reach करता है? अगले step में तुम guess करोगे।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ shape ఒక sine. tau equals m B sine theta. ఇది అత్యధిక value ఎక్కడ reach అవుతుంది? తర్వాత మీరు guess చేస్తారు.

STATE_6.title: Where is τ maximum? — predict before you see

scene_composition.text: At what angle is the torque MAXIMUM?

Common wrong guess:
θ = 0° (m lined up with B).
"Aligned must be biggest, right?"

Answer: θ = 90°.
τ = mB·sin 90° = mB.
A cross product is biggest
when the vectors are perpendicular.
τ = m × B. Parallel → sin 0 = 0 → τ = 0. Perpendicular → sin 90 = 1 → τ = max.

tts_sentences.text_en: The magnet is now frozen at ninety degrees. Look at the torque arrow — it is fully extended.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Magnet अभी ninety degrees पर frozen है। torque arrow को देखो — यह पूरी तरह extended है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Magnet ఇప్పుడు ninety degrees వద్ద frozen గా ఉంది. torque arrow చూడండి — అది పూర్తిగా extended గా ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Predict first. At what angle is the torque largest? Take three seconds.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पहले predict करो। torque किस angle पर सबसे बड़ा है? तीन seconds लो।
tts_sentences.text_te: మొదట predict చేయండి. torque ఏ angle వద్ద అత్యధికంగా ఉంటుంది? మూడు seconds తీసుకోండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Many students say zero degrees, where the moment m lines up with the field B. That feels right, but it is wrong. The answer is ninety.
tts_sentences.text_hi: बहुत से students कहते हैं zero degrees, जहाँ moment m, field B के साथ line up होता है। यह सही लगता है, लेकिन गलत है। answer ninety है।
tts_sentences.text_te: చాలా students zero degrees అంటారు, అక్కడ moment m, field B తో line up అవుతుంది. అది సరిగ్గా అనిపిస్తుంది, కానీ తప్పు. answer ninety.

tts_sentences.text_en: Because the torque tau is a cross product. Cross products are biggest when the two vectors are perpendicular, and zero when they are parallel.
tts_sentences.text_hi: क्योंकि torque tau एक cross product है। cross products सबसे बड़े होते हैं जब दोनों vectors perpendicular हों, और zero जब वे parallel हों।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఎందుకంటే torque tau ఒక cross product. cross products అత్యధికంగా ఉంటాయి రెండు vectors perpendicular గా ఉన్నప్పుడు, మరియు zero గా ఉంటాయి parallel గా ఉన్నప్పుడు.

misconception_watch.belief: Torque is maximum when m is parallel to B, because that is when the magnet is most aligned.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Alignment means τ = 0, not τ_max. τ = m × B is a cross product — it is largest when m ⊥ B (θ = 90°).

STATE_7.title: Stable vs unstable, U = −mB·cosθ, and the swing T = 2π√(I/mB)

scene_composition.text: Released near alignment, the magnet swings — like a compass

θ = 0° (m ∥ B): U is LOWEST.
Nudge it → it swings back.
Stable. It oscillates here
with period T = 2π√(I/mB).

θ = 180° (m against B): U is HIGHEST.
Any nudge grows → it flips around.
Unstable. See the U-meter on the left.
U = −mB·cosθ (lowest at 0°, highest at 180°). Swing period T = 2π√(I/mB).

tts_sentences.text_en: Torque is zero at two angles. Theta zero, where the moment m lines up with the field B, and theta one eighty, where m points against B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Torque दो angles पर zero है। theta zero, जहाँ moment m, field B के साथ line up होता है, और theta one eighty, जहाँ m, B के against point करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Torque రెండు angles వద్ద zero. theta zero వద్ద, అక్కడ moment m, field B తో line up అవుతుంది, మరియు theta one eighty వద్ద, అక్కడ m, B కి వ్యతిరేకంగా point చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: But they are not equally safe. Watch the magnet oscillate gently about theta zero. Nudge it and it always swings back.
tts_sentences.text_hi: लेकिन वे equally safe नहीं हैं। magnet को theta zero के आसपास gently oscillate होते देखो। इसे nudge करो और यह हमेशा वापस swing करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: కానీ అవి equally safe కాదు. magnet theta zero చుట్టూ gently oscillate అవ్వడం చూడండి. దాన్ని nudge చేస్తే అది ఎప్పుడూ తిరిగి swing అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is stable equilibrium. On the U-meter the marker sits at the bottom — the energy is lowest, U equals minus m B cosine theta.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह stable equilibrium है। U-meter पर marker नीचे sit करता है — energy सबसे कम है, U equals minus m B cosine theta।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది stable equilibrium. U-meter పై marker కింద ఉంది — energy అత్యల్పం, U equals minus m B cosine theta.

tts_sentences.text_en: Each gentle swing takes a time T equals two pi root I over m B. A stronger magnet or a stronger field makes it swing faster.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर gentle swing में समय लगता है T equals two pi root I over m B। एक मज़बूत magnet या मज़बूत field इसे तेज़ swing कराते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి gentle swing కి సమయం పడుతుంది T equals two pi root I over m B. ఒక బలమైన magnet లేదా బలమైన field దాన్ని వేగంగా swing చేస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Theta one eighty is the opposite — the energy maximum. Any tiny nudge grows and the magnet flips all the way to theta zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: theta one eighty उल्टा है — energy maximum। कोई भी tiny nudge बढ़ता है और magnet flip होकर theta zero पर आ जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: theta one eighty వ్యతిరేకం — energy maximum. ఏ tiny nudge అయినా పెరిగి magnet theta zero కి flip అవుతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: θ = 180° is just as stable as θ = 0°, because the torque is zero at both angles.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: Only θ = 0° is stable — the energy minimum. θ = 180° is the energy maximum, so any nudge grows and the magnet flips to θ = 0°.

misconception_watch.belief: The oscillation period does not depend on the magnet or the field.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: T = 2π√(I/mB): a stronger moment m or a stronger field B makes the swing FASTER (shorter period); a heavier magnet (larger I) makes it slower.

STATE_8.title: Your turn — drag m, B and θ; watch τ, U and the period T

scene_composition.text: Sandbox — drag the three sliders

τ = mB·sinθ
U = −mB·cosθ
T = 2π√(I/mB)

m or B up → τ up, T down

Try these:
1. Set θ = 90° — τ is biggest.
2. Set θ = 0° — τ = 0, U lowest.
3. Raise m and B — T gets shorter.

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. Three sliders — the magnetic moment m, the field B, and the angle theta. Drag them and watch the torque arrow respond.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब तुम्हारी बारी। तीन sliders — magnetic moment m, field B, और angle theta। इन्हें drag करो और torque arrow को respond करते देखो।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీ వంతు. మూడు sliders — magnetic moment m, field B, మరియు angle theta. వాటిని drag చేసి torque arrow respond అవ్వడం చూడండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Set theta to ninety for the biggest torque. Then raise the moment m or the field B and watch the swing period T get shorter.
tts_sentences.text_hi: सबसे बड़े torque के लिए theta को ninety पर set करो। फिर moment m या field B बढ़ाओ और swing period T को छोटा होते देखो।
tts_sentences.text_te: అత్యధిక torque కోసం theta ని ninety కి set చేయండి. తర్వాత moment m లేదా field B పెంచి swing period T తగ్గడం చూడండి.

STATE_1.label: A bar magnet and its moment m — no field yet
STATE_1.caption: A bar magnet (N red, S blue) with the magnetic moment m pointing S → N. The external field is not switched on yet.

STATE_2.label: Place it in a uniform field B (θ ≈ 60°)
STATE_2.caption: The uniform field B (identical blue arrows) is switched on. The magnet sits tilted at θ to the field; the yellow arc marks the angle.

STATE_3.label: The force couple — +mB and −mB
STATE_3.caption: Each pole feels a force mB: +mB along B on N, −mB against B on S. Equal, opposite, on different lines — a couple.

STATE_4.label: ΣF = 0, τ ≠ 0 — the magnet rotates toward B
STATE_4.caption: The two forces sum to zero (ΣF = 0 badge), yet the couple makes a torque τ. The magnet begins to rotate toward alignment.

STATE_5.label: θ sweep 0 → 180° — τ traces a sine
STATE_5.caption: θ animates 0 → 180° and back on a 10-second cycle. The τ arrow scales with sin θ — zero at 0° and 180°, biggest in the middle.

STATE_6.label: Frozen at θ = 90° — predict the maximum
STATE_6.caption: The magnet is pinned at θ = 90°, where τ = mB is at its maximum. Wrong-belief prompt and answer reveal via reveal_at_tts_id.

STATE_7.label: Stable oscillation about θ = 0° + U-meter + period T
STATE_7.caption: The magnet oscillates gently about θ = 0° (stable) with period T = 2π√(I/mB). The U-meter shows U = −mB·cosθ: lowest at 0°, highest at 180°.

STATE_8.label: Interactive sandbox — drag m, B, θ
STATE_8.caption: Sliders control m, B and θ. The τ arrow, the force couple, the U-meter and the readout (τ, U, period T) all update on every drag.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered this concept when they can explain that a bar magnet of moment m in a UNIFORM field B feels equal and opposite pole forces (+mB on N, −mB on S) that form a couple — zero net force, so the magnet does not translate — but a torque τ = m × B of magnitude mB·sinθ that rotates it toward alignment; that the torque is maximum at θ = 90° (a cross product is largest when the vectors are perpendicular) and zero at θ = 0° and 180°; that released near alignment it oscillates about θ = 0° with period T = 2π√(I/mB), so a stronger m or B makes it swing faster; and that the potential energy U = −m·B = −mB·cosθ is a minimum at θ = 0° (stable) and a maximum at θ = 180° (unstable), so the magnet always settles pointing along the field.

questions.stem: A bar magnet is placed in a uniform magnetic field. What does the field do to its two poles?

options.A: Each pole feels a force mB — +mB on N along B, −mB on S against B — equal and opposite on different lines, forming a couple
options.B: The poles feel no force, because they are equal and opposite
options.C: Both poles feel a force in the same direction, pushing the magnet bodily along B
options.D: Only the north pole feels a force; the south pole feels nothing

distractor_misconceptions.B: Thinks equal-and-opposite poles means no force at all, missing that each pole feels mB and they form a couple
distractor_misconceptions.C: Thinks the two pole forces add to a net force that translates the magnet (they are opposite, so they cancel)
distractor_misconceptions.D: Thinks only one pole interacts with the field

questions.tested_idea: Each pole feels a force mB; equal and opposite on different lines → a couple (ΣF = 0).
questions.parallel_form_stem: A compass needle sits in a uniform field. Describe the force on each of its poles and explain why the two forces form a couple rather than pushing it sideways.

questions.stem: In a uniform field the net force on a bar magnet is zero. Does that mean nothing happens to it?

options.A: No — the couple still produces a torque τ = m × B, so the magnet rotates toward alignment without translating
options.B: Yes — zero net force means the magnet stays completely still
options.C: No — the magnet slides along the field because the forces add up
options.D: Yes — a uniform field can never affect a magnet

distractor_misconceptions.B: Conflates zero net force with zero torque — misses that a couple turns the magnet
distractor_misconceptions.C: Thinks the pole forces produce translation (they cancel; only the torque acts)
distractor_misconceptions.D: Denies that a uniform field exerts any effect

questions.tested_idea: Zero net force but non-zero torque: the magnet rotates without translating.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A bar magnet released at an angle in a uniform field does not drift across the room. Explain what it does instead and why, in terms of force and torque.

questions.stem: At what angle between m and B is the torque on the magnet largest, and what is its value there?

options.A: At θ = 90°, where τ = mB·sin 90° = mB
options.B: At θ = 0°, where m is aligned with B
options.C: At θ = 180°, where m points opposite to B
options.D: The torque is the same at every angle

distractor_misconceptions.B: Thinks maximum torque is at alignment, where in fact τ = 0
distractor_misconceptions.C: Picks anti-alignment, where τ is also zero

questions.tested_idea: τ = mB·sinθ is maximum at θ = 90°, zero at 0° and 180°.
questions.parallel_form_stem: As a magnet turns from aligned to perpendicular in a uniform field, describe how the torque on it changes and state the angle of maximum torque.

questions.stem: Why is the torque on the magnet zero when its moment m is parallel to the field B?

options.A: Because τ = m × B is a cross product, which is zero when the two vectors are parallel (sin 0 = 0)
options.B: Because the field stops existing when the magnet is aligned
options.C: Because the pole forces become zero at alignment
options.D: Because the magnet's moment becomes zero at alignment

distractor_misconceptions.B: Invents a vanishing field rather than the sinθ factor
distractor_misconceptions.C: Thinks the pole forces vanish (they are still mB; their lines of action coincide so there is no couple)
distractor_misconceptions.D: Thinks the moment vanishes at alignment

questions.tested_idea: τ = m × B is a cross product → zero when m ∥ B (sin 0 = 0).
questions.parallel_form_stem: Explain, using the cross product τ = m × B, why a magnet already lined up with the field feels no turning effect.

questions.stem: A magnet rests aligned with the field (θ = 0°) and another rests anti-aligned (θ = 180°). How do these two equilibria differ?

options.A: θ = 0° is stable (U minimum, a nudge restores it); θ = 180° is unstable (U maximum, a nudge flips it to 0°)
options.B: Both are equally stable, since the torque is zero at both
options.C: θ = 180° is stable and θ = 0° is unstable
options.D: Neither is an equilibrium, because the field keeps turning the magnet

distractor_misconceptions.B: Equates zero torque with stability, ignoring the energy criterion
distractor_misconceptions.D: Denies that aligned/anti-aligned are equilibria at all

questions.tested_idea: U = −mB·cosθ: θ = 0° is the stable minimum, θ = 180° the unstable maximum.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Using the energy U = −mB·cosθ, explain which of the two zero-torque orientations a freely suspended magnet will actually settle into, and why.

questions.stem: A suspended bar magnet oscillates about the field direction with period T = 2π√(I/mB). If you replace it with a stronger magnet (larger m) in a stronger field (larger B), what happens to the period?

options.A: The period gets shorter — it swings faster, because T ∝ 1/√(mB)
options.B: The period gets longer — it swings slower
options.C: The period does not change; it is independent of m and B

distractor_misconceptions.B: Gets the dependence backwards (more mB → faster, not slower)
distractor_misconceptions.C: Thinks the period is independent of the magnet and field
distractor_misconceptions.D: Thinks a stronger field halts oscillation rather than speeding it

questions.tested_idea: T = 2π√(I/mB): a stronger m or B shortens the period (faster swing).
questions.parallel_form_stem: In a vibration magnetometer the magnet swings faster in a stronger field. Justify this from the period formula T = 2π√(I/mB).
